*Profit maximization analysis* begins with finding the optimal unit counts for each division to determine the true financial value entertainment.  
*Revenue and expense functions* for Division A yield a net profit component of \( 300x - 7x^2 \) after subtracting costs from income entertainment.  
*Division B calculations* show a net profit component of \( 250y - 7y^2 \) derived from its specific revenue and expense data entertainment.  
*Total profit derivation* combines both division results into a single function \( P(x, y) = 300x - 7x^2 + 250y - 7y^2 \) for optimization entertainment.  
*Optimal values determination* requires setting partial derivatives to zero which reveals that \( x \) equals approximately 21.43 units and \( y \) equals approximately 17.86 units entertainment.  
*Transfer price constraints* link the constants \( \alpha \) and \( \beta \) through the inequality \( 0.3(\alpha x - \beta y)n \leq 0.1 P(x, y) \) to limit tax liability entertainment.  
*Constant relationship establishment* shows that \( \alpha x - \beta y \) must not exceed one third of the total profit per transferred unit to satisfy the ten percent cap entertainment.  
*Final compliance check* ensures the firm adheres to tax regulations by using the derived value for \( \alpha \) and \( \beta \) in their transfer pricing model entertainment.
